headline: Ukraine strikes Russia's lifeblood by targeting oil ports
description: Long-range Ukrainian drones hit three key infrastructures for Russian crude oil exports, as Kyiv aims to limit Moscow's windfall from surging oil prices.

headline: France has not banned all military overflights to Israel and the Middle East
French trial examines Russian tax fraud scheme denounced by Magnitsky
Joël Soudron, one of France's most notorious drug traffickers, is arrested in Panama
description: Soudron was the subject of an Interpol Red Notice and two arrest warrants. He had been sought by the French National Fugitive Search Brigade since his escape in 2018.

headline: Israel introduces death penalty for Palestinians accused of 'terrorist' murders
description: The law, passed by 62 lawmakers to 48, marks turning point in Israel's penal policy and further weakens the rule of law in the country.

headline: Spice up your life! Five Paris restaurants where you can eat spicy food
description: Mexican chipotle, Korean kimchi, Tunisian harissa: Whether mild or fiery, these condiments add zest to dishes from around the world. Here are five addresses in Paris and Montreuil to awaken your taste buds.

headline: France has yet to find a forward-looking political discourse on racism
description: While Bally Bagayoko, the newly-elected mayor of Saint-Denis, has responded with composure to the media storm unleashed against him, leftist leader Jean-Luc Mélenchon is 'feeding the identity-based rhetoric of the far right,' writes Le Monde columnist Philippe Bernard.

Volodymyr Zelensky's exclusive interview with Le Monde on the war in Ukraine
description: In an interview with Le Monde, the Ukrainian president shares his perspective on a possible end to the conflict and urges the US and all allied nations to maintain their support for Ukraine.

headline: Assault, attempted murder, assassination: The rogue Freemasons going on trial in Paris
description: A coach, a race car driver, a trade unionist: Nothing seemed to connect these otherwise unremarkable individuals. Yet all were targeted by the same criminal organization. At its head were prominent members of a Masonic lodge in a Paris suburb. These 'brothers,' now enemies, are standing trial from March 30.

headline: Escaping Putin's Russia: The father and daughter persecuted over a drawing
description: Alexei Moskalyov and his daughter, Maria, are symbolic victims of the Kremlin's repression. The father was jailed after posting an anti-war drawing his teenage daughter made at school. They have just arrived in France and are under humanitarian protection.
